DEFINITIONS OF CERTAIN TERMS 
Words and terms used in this summary of the 2018B Bonds Indentu re are defined therein and 
shall have the same meaning herein as in the respective legal d ocuments. Certain of these words and 
terms are defined below for convenience. 
“Additional Bonds ” means any series of Bonds other than the 1996 Bonds authenticated and 
delivered pursuant to the 2018B Bonds Indenture, as described u nder “THE 2018B BONDS 
INDENTURE - Additional Bonds.” 
“Agreement” means the Turnkey Installment Sale Agreement between One Park way Inc. and the 
Authority. 
“Authority” means the Philadelphia Authority for Industrial Development. 
“Bond Counsel” means an attorney or a law firm appointed by the Authority having a national 
reputation in the field of municipal law whose opinions are generally accepted by purchasers of municipal 
obligations. 
“Bond Fund” means the fund so designated pursuant to the 2018B Bonds Indenture. 
“Bond Insurer ” means with respect to any seri es of Additional Bonds, the ent ity, if any, that 
issues a bond insurance policy guaranteeing such series. 
“Bonds” means the Prior Bonds, the 2018B Bonds and all other bonds is sued, authenticated and 
delivered pursuant to the 2018B Bonds Indenture. (The 1996 Bond s are no longer Outstanding under the 
2018B Bonds Indenture.) 
“Bondholder” or “holder of Bonds” means the registered owner of any Bond. 
“Capital Additions ” means property of any kind and all additions, improvements or 
extraordinary repairs to the Leased Premises acquired or constr ucted by the Authority or by or for the 
City after November 1. 1996. except for work to be performed un der the Agreement as defined in the 
Original Indenture.  E -2 
  
“City” means the City of Philadelphia. 
“Commonwealth” means the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania. 
“Consulting Engineer” means, for purposes of the Lease, an engineer or architect (o r firm or 
corporation thereof) registered in the Commonwealth or another state to the extent permitted by law, and 
qualified to pass upon construction questions, who has been appointed by the City or the Authority, as the 
case may be, and is not unsatisfactory to the other party or the Trustee and who is, in fact, independent. 
“Counsel” means an attorney-at-law or law firm (who may be the City Solicitor or other counsel 
for the City or counsel for the Authority) licensed to practice  law and in good standing in the 
Commonwealth. 
 “First Supplemental Indenture” means the First Supplemental Trust Indenture dated as of 
December 1, 2007 between the Authority and the 2018B Bonds Trustee. 
“Government Obligations” means obligations to the timely payment of which the full fai th and 
credit of the United States is pledged.   
 “Investment Securities” means any of the following securities, if and to the extent t he same arc 
at the time legal for the investment of Authority funds: 
(a) Government Obligations; 
(b) Government Obligations which have been stripped of their unmatu red interest 
coupons and interest coupons stripped from Government Obligations (stripped securities are only 
permitted if they have been stri pped by the federal agency that  issued or guaranteed the Government 
Obligation); 
(c) Bonds, debentures, notes or other evidence of indebtedness issu ed or guaranteed 
by any of the following: Federal Home Loan Bank System. Federal  National Mortgage Association. 
Government National Mortgage Association. Farmers Home Administ ration. Federal Financing Bank, 
Federal Housing Administration Debentures, General Services Adm inistration, U.S. Maritime 
Administration. U.S. Housing and Urban Development, Federal Hom e Loan Mortgage Corporation, 
Student Loan Marketing Association. Resolution Funding Corporation, Export-Import Bank of the United 
States, or Farm Credit System; 
(d) Interest-bearing time or dema nd deposits, certificates of depos it, or other similar 
banking arrangements with any government securities dealer, ban k, trust company, savings and loan 
association, national banking associ ation or other savings inst itution (including the 2018B Bonds Trustee 
and its affiliates), provided that such deposits, certificates, and other arrangements arc fully insured by the 
Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation or any successor federal deposit insurance corporation or entity; 
(e) Repurchase agreements collateralized by securities described in subparagraphs 
(a), (b). or obligations described in (c) above which are backe d by the full faith and credit of the United 
States Government with any registered broker/dealer subject to the Securities Investors’ Protection 
Corporation or with any commercial bank (in either case, includ ing the 2018B Bonds Trustee and its 
affiliates), provided that (1) a specific written repurchase ag reement governs the transaction, (2) the 
securities, free and clear of any lien, are held by the municip al entity, the 2018B Bonds Trustee (if the 
2018B Bonds Trustee is not supplying the collateral) or an inde pendent third party acting solely as agent 
for the 2018B Bonds Trustee (if the 2018B Bonds Trustee is supplying the collateral), and such third  E -3 
  
party is (a) a Federal Reserve Bank, or (b) a bank which is a m ember of the Federal Deposit Insurance 
Corporation and which has a combined capital, surplus and undiv ided profits of not less than S25 million, 
and the 2018B Bonds Trustee shall have received written confirmation from such third party that it holds 
such securities, free and clear o f any lien, as agent for the 2 018B Bonds Trustee. (3) a perfected first 
security interest under the Uniform Commercial Code, or book entry procedures described in 31 CFR 
306.1, el seq.  or 31 CFR 350.0,  el seq.,  in such securities is created for the benefit of the 2018B Bon ds 
Trustee. (4) the repurchase agreement has a term of thirty days  or less, or the 2018B Bonds Trustee will 
value the collateral securities no less frequently than weekly marked to market at current price plus 
accrued interest and will liquidate the collateral securities if any deficiency in the required collateral 
percentage is not restored within two Business Days of such val uation, (5) the fair market value of the 
collateral securities in relation to the amount of the repurchase obligation, including principal and interest, 
is equal to at least 104% (if the securities used as collateral are Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation 
or Federal National Mortgage Association Securities, then the v alue of the collateral securities shall equal 
at least 105%), (6) the collateral was not acquired by the broker/dealer pursuant to a repurchase 
agreement or reverse repurchase agreement, and (7) a legal opinion of Counsel is delivered to the 
municipal entity and the 2018B Bonds Trustee that the repurchas e agreements comply with the guidelines 
under state law for legal investment of public funds; 
(f) Repurchase agreements with maturities beyond 30 days acceptable  to the Bond 
Insurer: 
(g) Money market funds including shares of a fund for which the 201 8B Bonds 
Trustee or an affiliate performs services for a fee under the F ederal Investment Company Act of 1940, 
whose shares are registered under the Federal Securities Act of 1933, and rated “AAAm-G” or “AAA-m” 
or “AA-m” by S&P and. if rated by Moody’s, rated “Aaa,” “Aal” or “Aa2.” 
(h) Commercial paper rated “Prime-1” or better by Moody’s or “A-1” or better by 
S&P; 
(i) Obligations rated by Moody’s and S&P in one of the two highest rating 
categories assigned by such agencies; 
(j) Advance-Refunded Municipal Bonds; 
(k) Tax-exempt obligations that are rated by Moody’s and S&P in one  o f  t h e  t w o  
highest rating categories assigned by such agencies, or shares of investment companies that invest only in 
such obligations; 
(l) The 2018B Bonds Trustee’s “cash sweep account” or other short term investment 
fund of the 2018B Bonds Trustee or its subsidiaries, the assets of which consist of other Investment 
Securities defined above; and 
(m) Investment agreements acceptable to the Bond Insurer.

Franklin Parkway, as more fully described in Exhibit A-l attached to the Lease.

“1996 Project” below) and all amendments, modifications, renewals and supplements thereto.  E -4

Authority and the 2018B Bonds Trustee.

and delivered, as defined in the Original Indenture.

Series C (City of Philadelphia - One Benjamin Franklin Parkway Project).

Bond plus the applicable premium, if any, payable upon redempti on thereof pursuant to the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture.

April 1, 2018 between the Authority and the 2018B Bonds Trustee.

required to redeem Bonds of a series pursuant to mandatory sinking fund redemption.

Trust Company, N. A. (successor to Mellon Bank. N.A.).

Project). (The 1996 Bonds are no longer Outstanding under the 2018B Bonds Indenture.)

consisting of (a) the acquisition of land located at 1515-29 Arch Street. 1532 Cherry Street and 117-125 
North Sixteenth Street, commonly known as the One Benjamin Fran klin Parkway as more fully described 
in Exhibit A-l attached to the Lease (the “Land”); (b) the acquisition of the Air Rights Estate (as described 
and defined in the Agreement) pursuant to the Turnkey Installme nt Sale Agreement between One 
Parkway Inc., a Pennsylvania corporation, as seller, (the “Sell er”) and the Authority, as purchaser, as 
amended and supplemented from ti me to time (the “Agreement”), ( c) the acquisition of furniture, fixtures 
and equipment to be placed in the Leased Premises (as defined i n the Lease) for lease to the City; and (d) 
the leasing of the Land and Air Rights Estate pursuant to the Lease between the Authority and the City (as 
amended, modified, renewed and supplemented from time to time, the “Lease”); and (e) the payment of 
costs of issuance of the Prior Bonds.

accordance with the terms thereof at the time in question, incl uding by the First Supplemental Indenture 
and the Second Supplemental Indenture.

THE 2018B BONDS INDENTURE 
Pledge and Assignment 
Pursuant to the 2018B Bonds Indenture the Authority pledges and  grants to the 2018B Bonds 
Trustee a security interest in all of the Authority’s right, ti tle and interest in and to the Lease (except for 
the Authority’s rights with respect to indemnity and the Author ity’s obligations which can be performed 
by only the Authority), the Pledged Revenues (defined as the revenues received by the Authority from the 
City pursuant to the Lease, except the sums payable in connection with the Authority’s rights with respect 
to indemnity, and any and all amounts payable to the Authority on account of the Leased Premises and all 
funds and income and receipts on the funds held by the 2018B Bo nds Trustee thereunder) and all moneys 
and securities held from time to time by the 2018B Bonds Truste e under the 2018B Bonds Indenture, 
subject to the terms thereof. 
Pursuant to a separate Assignment, the Authority has assigned a ll of its right, title and interest in 
and to the Lease and all moneys payable thereunder (except for the right to indemnification thereunder) to 
the 2018B Bonds Trustee. 
Acquisition Fund 
The 2018B Bonds Trustee has established an Acquisition Fund for  the payment of the costs of 
acquisition of the Leased Premises.  (No balance exists in the Acquisition Fund and no deposits are 
expected to be made to the Acquisition Fund in connection with the issuance of the 2018B Bonds.)  
Bond Fund; Application of Bond Fund 
The 2018B Bonds Trustee has established a Bond Fund and three (3) separate accounts therein 
designated, respectively, the “Interest Account,” the “Principa l Account” and the “Bond Redemption 
Account.” 
Payments made under the Lease (ex cept such amounts as shall pro vide certain indemnification 
from the City to the Authority) are deposited in the Bond Fund.  While any Bonds are Outstanding, 
moneys from the Bond Fund are used solely for the payment of th e principal or Redemption Price of and 
interest on the Bonds as the same shall become due and payable at maturity, upon redemption or 
otherwise.  Any amounts remaining in the Bond Fund, after payment in full of the principal or 
Redemption Price of and interest on the Bonds (or provision for the payment thereof), and payment in full 
of the fees, charges and expenses of the Authority and the 2018B Bonds Trustee, are paid to the City. 
Funds for the payment of the principal of or Redemption Price of and interest on the Bonds are 
derived from moneys furnished by the Authority or the City to t he 2018B Bonds Trustee, including 
moneys received by the 2018B Bonds Trustee pursuant to the Lease. 
Procedure When Funds Are Sufficient to Pay All Bonds  
If at any time the amounts held by the 2018B Bonds Trustee in the funds established under the 
2018B Bonds Indenture are sufficient to pay principal and inter est on all Bonds then Outstanding at 
maturity or by prior redemption, together with any amounts due the 2018B Bonds Trustee, the 2018B 
Bonds Trustee shall notify the Authority to that effect and the reafter the 2018B Bonds Trustee shall apply 
the amounts in the funds and accounts established under the 201 8B Bonds Indenture to the payment of 
such principal and interest and neither the City nor the Author ity shall be required to pay over any further 
Pledged Revenues unless and until it shall appear that there is  a deficiency in the funds held by the 2018B  E -6 
  
Bonds Trustee. If the 2018B Bonds Trustee has applied the funds  to pay the full principal of and interest 
due on the Bonds and any other incidental expenses incurred to make such payment, the 2018B Bonds 
Trustee shall transfer any remaining funds to the City. 
Moneys to Be Held for All Bondholders With Certain Exceptions 
Moneys and investments in the various funds and accounts established under the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture shall, until applied as provided with respect to the fund in question, be held in trust by the 
2018B Bonds Trustee for the benefit of the holders of all Outstanding Bonds, except any bond redemption 
fund established for any series of Bonds shall be held for the benefit of the holders of Bonds of that series 
only and the Rebate Fund. 
Investment of Funds 
All moneys received by the 2018B Bonds Trustee under the 2018B Bonds Indenture for deposit 
in any Fund established thereunder are considered trust funds, are not subject to lien or attachment other 
than the lien of the 2018B Bonds Indenture and are, except as otherwise provided in the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture, are deposited in the c ommercial department of the 20 18B Bonds Trustee, until or unless 
invested or deposited as provided in the 2018B Bonds Indenture.   All deposits in the commercial 
department of the 2018B Bonds Trustee shall, to the extent not insured by the Federal Deposit Insurance 
Corporation, be fully secured by a pledge of Government Obligations or secured as required by 
Pennsylvania law for deposits of trust funds. 
The 2018B Bonds Trustee shall as directed by the City, invest m oneys held in the Funds 
established under the 2018B Bonds Indenture in Investment Securities. The interest and other income 
received upon such investments and any interest paid by the 201 8B Bonds Trustee or any other 
depository of any fund or account established under the 2018B Bonds Indenture and any profit (or losses) 
resulting from the sale of securities shall be added or charged  to the respective funds and accounts 
established under the 2018B Bonds Indenture. 
Neither the 2018B Bonds Trustee nor the Authority shall be acco untable for any depreciation in 
the value of any such security or for any loss resulting from t he sale thereof. If the net proceeds from the 
sale of securities shall be less than the amount invested, such  deficit shall be made up from the Bond 
Fund. 
Valuation of Funds 
In computing the assets of any fund or account, investments and accrued interest thereon shall be 
deemed a part thereof. Such investments shall be valued by the 2018B Bonds Trustee annually on June 
30, at the current market value thereof, or at the redemption price thereof, if then redeemable at the option 
of the holder.  E -7 
  
Additional Bonds 
The Authority may. but is not compelled to, issue additional series of bonds (“Additional Bonds”) 
from time to time under the 2018B Bonds Indenture on a parity w ith the Bonds (a) to pay costs of 
completing the Project or Capital Additions and (b) to refund b onds. The Lease requires that rental 
payments be made at times and in amounts sufficient to make deb t service payments when due on all 
bonds outstanding from time to time under the 2018B Bonds Inden ture, including any Additional Bonds.  
Additional Bonds may be issued upon receipt by the 2018B Bonds Trustee of the following documents, 
among others.  
General Requirements. In all cases, the following shall be delivered to the 2018B Bonds Trustee: 
(i) executed counterparts of such documents as are necessary or appropriate 
in order to provide, inter alia, for the issuance of the Additi onal Bonds and the security therefor, 
the application of Bond proceeds and other available moneys, an d the payment of the principal or 
Redemption Price of and interest on the Additional Bonds, together with appropriate Authority 
and City certificates to the effect that the foregoing documents have been duly and validly 
authorized, executed and delivered by the Authority and the City: 
(ii) certified resolutions of the Authority and ordinances of the Ci ty 
approving the issuance of the Additional Bonds and the executio n and delivery of all documents 
and the taking of all actions which are necessary or appropriate in connection with the issuance of 
the Additional Bonds; 
(iii) an opinion or opinions of Counsel to the effect that: (A) the Additional 
Bonds have been duly issued for a permitted purpose under the 2 018B Bonds Indenture; (B) all 
consents or approvals required to be obtained from any regulatory body for the issuance of the 
Additional Bonds have been obtained; (C) the issuance of the Ad ditional Bonds and execution 
and delivery of related documents will not constitute a breach o r  d e f a u l t  o n  t h e  p a r t  o f  t h e  
Authority or the City under, as applicable, any related party’s articles of incorporation or by-laws, 
any applicable laws or regulations, court orders or rulings of regulatory bodies to which the 
Authority or the City is subject or any agreements to which the  Authority or the City is a party or 
to which the Authority’s properties arc subject: (D) all documents delivered by the Authority and 
the City in connection with the issuance of the Additional Bond s have been duly and validly 
authorized, executed and delivered and such execution and deliv ery and all other actions taken by 
the Authority in connection with the issuance of the Additional  Bonds have been duly authorized 
by all necessary corporate actions; and (E) all conditions prec edent to the issuance of the 
Additional Bonds pursuant to the 2018B Bonds Indenture have been satisfied; 
(iv) an opinion of bond counsel that the issuance of such Additional Bonds 
will not adversely affect the exclusion from gross income of interest on the Bonds Outstanding 
(i.e., for federal income tax purposes); 
(v) a certificate of the Authority (which may rely upon a certifica te of the 
Director of Finance of the City with respect to the amount of t he rentals payable under the Lease) 
demonstrating and concluding tha t the rentals payable under the Lease in each Bond Year 
(commencing with the Bond Year in which such series of Bonds is  issued) will not be less than 
the Annual Debt Service Requirement on all Bonds Outstanding in  each such Bond Year 
(including the Bonds of the series then proposed to be issued);  
  E -8 
  
Additional Requirements - Capital Additions. The following shal l be delivered to the 2018B 
Bonds Trustee in the case of Additional Bonds issued to provide  funds for the Cost of undertaking or 
completing a Capital Addition: 
(i) executed counterparts or certified copies of construction contr acts or 
construction manager contracts (including all change orders or amendments then in effect) 
covering all construction or renovation work which is not to be  undertaken by employees of the 
Authority or the City, together with all required surety bonds and policies or certificates of 
insurance together with certificates to the effect that the Authority and the City have each duly 
and validly authorized, executed and delivered the construction  contracts or construction manager 
contracts, if any, for the Capital Addition: 
(ii) in the case of any Capital Addition involving construction, an Architect’s 
certificate: (A) stating that the Capital Addition can be under taken and completed in accordance 
with sound architectural and engineering practices and that all  necessary plans and specifications 
therefor have been approved by the Architect; (B) stating that all permits and approvals then 
required at the time to be in effect for the Capital Addition have been obtained and no facts or 
circumstances are known to the Architect which would prevent th e timely issuance of all other 
necessary permits and approvals; and (C) setting forth in reaso nable detail the items of Cost 
relating to construction or renovation work and stating that such items of Cost are reasonable: 
(iii) an Ordinance of the City approving the Capital Addition and any  
construction contracts, purchase agreements, or construction manager contracts and plans and 
specifications therefor; 
(iv) a certificate of the City: (A) setting forth in reasonable detail the Costs of 
the Capital Addition, including the items of Cost set forth in the Architect’s certificate described 
above, equipment costs, financing costs and other related fees and expenses; and (B) 
demonstrating the adequacy of the proceeds of the Additional Bonds and other available funds for 
the payment of all Costs of the Capital Addition; and 
(v) an opinion or opinions of Counsel: (A) stating that the City ha s duly and 
validly authorized, executed and delivered the construction con t r a c t s ,  i f  a n y .  f o r  t h e  C a p i t a l  
Addition; (B) stating that the Authority has acquired or will a cquire good and marketable title to 
or a valid and enforceable real property interest in all proper ty to be acquired or constructed as 
pan of the Capital Addition (which opinion may be given in reli ance upon a title insurance policy 
or an information certificate issued by a reputable title insur ance company); and (C) to the same 
effect, with respect to permits and approvals, as the Architect ’s Certificate described in 
subparagraph (ii) above. 
Insofar as an amendment or supplement to the Lease is requisite for the issuance of 
Additional Bonds, the City and the Authority have executed such amendment or supplement. 
 
Additional Requirements - Refunding Bonds. The following shall be delivered to the 2018B 
Bonds Trustee in connection with the issuance of Additional Bonds for the purpose of a refunding: 
(i) executed counterparts of such documents as are necessary or appropriate 
for the purposes of the refunding, including, if appropriate, a n escrow agreement or supplemental 
indenture providing for the deposit and application of funds for the refunding and irrevocable 
instructions from the Authority with respect to the redemption of refunded Bonds;  E -9 
  
(ii) a certified resolution of the Authority and an Ordinance of the  City 
authorizing the refunding, including, without limitation theret o, the redemption of refunded 
Bonds; and 
(iii) an Authority certificate and City certificate setting forth in reasonable 
detail the costs of the refunding and demonstrating the adequacy of available funds for the 
payment of such costs and. unless all refunded Bonds are to be redeemed or otherwise retired on 
the date of settlement for the Additional Bonds, such certificates shall be accompanied by such 
schedules, verified as to mathematical accuracy by a Certified Public Accountant, as are 
necessary to demonstrate the adequacy of the proceeds of the Ad ditional Bonds and other 
available funds deposited for the refunding and the income thereon for the purpose of paying, 
when due, the principal or Redemption Price of and interest on the refunded Bonds. 
Covenants of Authority 
Payment of Principal or Redemption Price of and Interest on Bonds. The Authority shall 
promptly pay or cause to be paid the principal or applicable Re demption Price of, and interest on every 
Bond issued under the 2018B Bonds Indenture according to the te rms thereof, but shall be required to 
make such payment only out of its Pledged Revenues.  
Corporate Existence and Maintenance of Properties. The Authority shall maintain its corporate 
existence throughout the term of the Bonds; and shall commit no  act which would impair the exemption 
from Federal income taxation of interest income derived from th e Bonds pursuant to the Code, as 
amended, and the regulations the reunder, consistent with the la w applicable thereto on the date of the 
original delivery of the Bonds; shall use its best efforts to m aintain and renew all its rights, powers, 
privileges and franchises to provide for continuous operation of the Leased Premises; and shall comply 
with all valid and applicable laws, acts, rules, regulations, permits, orders, requirements and directions of 
any legislative, executive, administrative or judicial body. 
Compliance with Lease. The Authority covenants and agrees that (i) it shall comply with all 
applicable provisions of the Lease, as if contained within the 2018B Bonds Indenture; and (ii) it shall 
enforce against the City the obligations of the City under the Lease, including, without limitation, the 
obligation to pay the rental payments when due. 
Leasing, etc. of Leased Premises. Subject to the provisions of the 2018B Bonds Indenture 
described below under “Covenants of Authority – Sale or Encumbr ance of Leased Premises Prohibited; 
Exceptions,” the Authority may, with the consent of the City, l ease the Leased Premised or any portions 
thereof to, or enter into operating agreements in respect there of with, third parties lawfully entitled to 
enter into such leases or agreements, but the Authority covenan ts that any such lease or agreement shall 
be subject to, and shall not contain terms inconsistent with, t he 2018B Bonds Indenture and shall be 
approved by the 2018B Bonds Tru stee. The Authority agrees that,  except for the assignment of the Lease 
to the 2018B Bonds Trustee pursuant to the 2018B Bonds Indentur e, it will not assign or otherwise 
encumber the Lease or any payments due thereunder. 
Enforcement. Execution and Amendment of Lease and Other Contrac ts: Notice of Default. The 
Authority shall cause the 1996 Project to be acquired or constr ucted and installed in accordance with the 
terms of the Lease, the Agreement, and the 2018B Bonds Indentur e and shall enforce payment of service 
charges, rentals and other amounts payable under the Lease or s upplement or amendment thereto, and any 
other lease, agreement or other contract executed pursuant to the provisions described in the preceding 
paragraph under the subcaption “Leasing, etc. of Lease Premises ” or otherwise made subject to the lien 
and security interest created by the 2018B Bonds Indenture, and  shall otherwise enforce all of its rights  E-10 
  
and privileges, and honor all of its obligations thereunder, and shall require every party with which it so 
contracts to perform its contractual obligations. So long as no Event of Default under the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture shall have occurred and be continuing, the Authority may exercise all of its rights under the 
Lease, or any other lease, sublease, agreement or contract, or supplement or amendment thereto, but the 
Authority shall not, without the consent of the 2018B Bonds Tru stee (and the Bond Insurer, if applicable) 
amend any of the same so as to affect adversely the Authority’s  ability to perform its covenants under the 
2018B Bonds Indenture. The Authorit y shall file with the 2018B Bonds Trustee copies of the Lease, and 
all other leases, agreements and contracts of the nature referr ed to in the provisions described in the 
preceding paragraph under the subcaption “Leasing, etc. of Leased Premises” (whether theretofore or 
thereafter executed), together with all amendments or supplemen ts thereto, whether or not the 2018B 
Bonds Trustee’s consent is required thereto, and shall give prompt notice to the 2018B Bonds Trustee of 
any default by any of the parties thereto except defaults of mi nor importance to the interests of the 
Authority therein. 
Property Insurance. So long as the Leased Premises are subject to the terms of the Lease, the City 
may act as self-insurer and the Authority need not obtain prope rty insurance; otherwise, the Authority 
shall keep the Leased Premises, or cause the same to be kept, i nsured by one or more insurance 
companies authorized and qualified to do business under the laws of the Commonwealth against such 
risks as are normally insured against; provided that the amount  of such insurance shall be sufficient to 
prevent diminution of recovery for any loss thereunder by reaso n of any co-insurance clause in the 
policies therefor unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Bond Insurer (if applicable). 
Each policy of insurance, if any, applicable to the Leased Premises may contain a loss deductible 
clause specifying such sum or sums, as the Authority may determ ine as the sum or sums, to be deducted 
from the amount of loss resulting from particular perils. All i nsurance policies, other than workers’ 
compensation insurance, shall be nonassessable and shall be wri tten in the names of the 2018B Bonds 
Trustee and the Authority (and, where appropriate, the lessee o r similarly interested party), as their 
interests may appear and shall be made payable to the 2018B Bon ds Trustee, which shall have the sole 
right to receive the proceeds of such policies and to collect a ny receipts for claims thereunder. So far as 
the same may be procurable at a commercially reasonable cost wi thout the payment of additional 
premiums, the policies for such insurance shall not be cancellable without at least thirty (30) days’ written 
notice to the Authority and the 2018B Bonds Trustee and shall p rovide that all losses thereunder shall be 
payable to the 2018B Bonds Trustee notwithstanding any act or neglect of the Authority which might 
otherwise result in a forfeiture of such insurance. Any insuran ce proceeds shall be held by the 2018B 
Bonds Trustee as security for all Bonds then Outstanding until paid out as described below. 
Any insurance policies if required under the 2018B Bonds Indent ure, or copies thereof or 
insurance certificates with respect thereto, shall be filed wit h the 2018B Bonds Trustee; provided, 
however, that the 2018B Bonds Trustee shall be under no obligation to determine whether policies of 
insurance need to be deposited with the 2018B Bonds Trustee or to confirm that such policies have been 
deposited with the 2018B Bonds Trustee and comply with the requ irements of the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture described under this sub-caption. 
Application of Proceeds of Property Insurance and Condemnation Awards. In the event of any 
damage to the Leased Premises covered by insurance or condemnat ion of any portion of the Leased 
Premises, the Authority shall promptly notify the 2018B Bonds Trustee in writing of such event. The 
proceeds of property insurance and condemnation awards shall be  applied pursuant to the provisions of 
the Lease.  E-11 
  
If the insurance proceeds or condemnation awards are insufficient to pay the cost of repair, 
reconstruction or replacement, the deficiency shall be supplied at the request of the Authority out of funds 
payable by the City as additional rent or otherwise. 
If any insurance proceeds or condemnation proceeds are not so a pplied as set forth in the Lease 
within twelve (12) months after receipt by the 2018B Bonds Trus tee or the City, or if the Authority fails 
to file or cause to be filed the required certificate with respect to any damage to the Leased Premises 
within three (3) months after the occurrence of the condemnatio n, injury or damage, or if the certificate 
which is filed states that the repair, reconstruction or replac ement is not practical or desirable, the 
insurance proceeds or condemnation awards shall be deposited in the Bond Fund to be applied as set forth 
in the Lease. 
Public Liability and Property Damage Insurance. The Authority will carry, or cause to be carried, 
liability and property damage ins urance in such amounts and in such manner as is set forth in the Lease 
or, if the Lease has been terminated, as is customary in similar operations. 
Sale or Encumbrance of Leased Premises Prohibited: Exceptions.  Except as permitted by the 
Lease or as otherwise specifically permitted by the 2018B Bonds  Indenture, the Authority shall not sell, 
lease, pledge or otherwise dispose of the Leased Premises or an y part thereof or of the revenues derived 
therefrom or create any lien or encumbrance thereon. Upon the c onditions stated, and upon receipt of any 
document required under the Lease, the 2018B Bonds Trustee shal l execute such consents or releases to 
any sale, transfer or encumbrance permitted thereby as may be appropriate. 
Payment of Taxes: Discharge of Liens. The Authority shall pay, or cause to be paid, all taxes, 
assessments, or other municipal or governmental charges lawfully imposed upon the Authority by virtue 
of its ownership of the Leased Premises or any revenues therefr om and will not suffer to be created or to 
exist any lien or charge thereon except the lien and charge of the 2018B Bonds Indenture and 
encumbrances permitted under the 2018B Bonds Indenture or under the Lease. 
Extension of Time for Payment of Interest Prohibited. The Autho rity shall not directly or 
indirectly extend or assent to the extension of the time for payment of any claim for interest on, any of the 
Bonds and shall not directly or indirectly be a party to or app rove any arrangement therefor by purchasing 
or funding or in any manner keeping alive any such claim for interest; and no claim for interest which in 
any way at or after maturity shall have been transferred or ple dged apart from the Bond to which it relates 
or which shall in any manner have been kept alive after maturit y by extension or by  purchase thereof by 
or on behalf of the Authority  shall be entitled, in case of a default under the 2018B Bonds Indenture, to 
any benefit or security under the 2018B Bonds Indenture except after the prior payment in full of the 
principal of all Bonds and claims for interest appertaining thereto not so transferred, pledged, kept alive or 
extended. 
Further Assurances: Additional Revenues. The Authority shall no t enter into any contract or take 
any action by which the rights of the 2018B Bonds Trustee or th e holders of the Bonds may be impaired 
and shall, from time to time, execute and deliver such further instruments and take such further action as 
may be required to carry out t he purposes of the 2018B Bonds Indenture. If at any time the Authority 
receives any income or payment from or in respect of the Leased Premises, it shall promptly pay the same 
to the 2018B Bonds Trustee for deposit in the Bond Fund and, at  the request of the 2018B Bonds Trustee, 
shall execute and deliver an assignment of its right, title and  interest in and to future income or payments 
of the same type to the 2018B Bonds Trustee to be held as part of Pledged Revenues and file or record 
such assignment as may be appropriate to perfect the security interest created. 
  E-12 
  
Events of Defaults and Remedies   
Events of Default Defined.  Each of following events will const itute an “Event of Default’’ under 
the 2018B Bonds Indenture: 
(a) a failure to pay the principal or redemption price of any Bond when it becomes 
due and payable at maturity or upon call for redemption; or 
(b) failure to pay any installment of interest on any Bond when it becomes due and 
payable; or 
(c) the occurrence of any “event of  default” under the payment sect ions of the Lease 
or the occurrence of any of the events enumerated in the event of default section of the Lease relating to 
bankruptcy or insolvency; or 
(d) the occurrence of any “event of default” under the Lease, other than (c) above, or 
a default in paying the rentals or other payments under any oth er lease or operating agreement or any 
amendment or supplement thereto relating to the Leased Premises  or any other default thereunder which 
would give the Authority the right to terminate such lease or agreement; or 
(e) a failure by the Authority to comply with any provision of the Act, which failure 
continues for a period of thirty (30) days, or in the event that the Authority is, for any reason, rendered 
incapable of fulfilling its obligations under the 2018B Bonds Indenture or the Act; or 
(f) a default by the Authority in the due and punctual performance of any other 
covenant in the Bonds or in the  2018B Bonds Indenture and failure to remedy the same within thirty (30) 
days (or such longer period as may be permitted under the 2018B  Bonds Indenture) after notice given 
pursuant to the 2018B Bonds Indenture. 
Acceleration and Annulment Thereof.  Upon the occurrence of an Event of Default under (a), (b) 
or (c) above, the 2018B Bonds Trustee shall, and if any other E vent of Default occurs, may, and upon the 
request of the owners of 25% in aggregate principal amount of all Bonds then Outstanding shall, by notice 
in writing to the Authority (but only with the prior written co nsent of the Bond Insurer if it is not in 
default in its payment obligation under the related bond insura nce policy, if applicable), declare the 
principal of all Bonds then Outs tanding to be immediately due a nd payable; and upon such declaration, 
such principal together with interest accrued thereon shall become and be immediately due and payable. 
Enforcement by Trustee.   If any Event of Default under the 2018B Bonds Indenture occurs and is 
continuing, the 2018B Bonds Trustee, before or after declaring the principal of the Bonds immediately 
due and payable, may enforce each and every right granted to it under the Lease. 
If, after the principal of Bonds has been so declared to be due  and payable, all arrears of interest 
upon the Bonds (and interest on overdue installments of interest at the rate (if any) provided in the Bonds) 
are paid by the Authority, and the Authority performs all other  things in respect to which it may have 
been in default under the 2018B Bonds Indenture, and pays the r easonable charges and expenses of the 
2018B Bonds Trustee, the holders of the Bonds and any trustee appointed under the Act, including 
reasonable attorneys’ fees. then, and in every such case, the Holders of a majority in aggregate principal 
amount of the Bonds then outsta nding by written notice to the A uthority and the 2018B Bonds Trustee, 
(with the prior written consent of the Bond Insurer, as applica ble, if it is not in default in its payment 
obligation under the related bo nd insurance policy), may annul such declaration of acceleration and its 
consequences, and such annulment shall be binding upon the 2018 B Bonds Trustee and upon all holders  E-13 
  
of the Bonds, but no such annulment shall extend to or affect a ny subsequent default or impair any right 
or remedy consequent thereon. 
Legal Proceedings by Trustee. If any Event of Default has occurred and is continuing, and has not 
been waived or cured, the 2018B Bonds Trustee, but only with th e prior written consent of the Bond 
Insurer (if applicable), if it is not in default in its payment  obligation under the related bond insurance 
policy in its discretion may, and upon the written request of t he holders of 25% in aggregate principal 
amount of the Bonds then Outstanding and receipt of indemnity to its satisfaction shall, in its own name: 
(a) by mandamus, or other suit, action or proceeding at law or in e quity, enforce all 
rights of the Bondholders, and to require the Authority to carr y out any other agreements with, or for the 
benefit of, the Bondholders and to perform its or their duties under the law; 
(b) bring suit upon the Bonds or the Lease; 
(c) by action or suit in equity require the Authority to account as if it were the 2018B 
Bonds Trustee of an express trust for the Bondholders and the Bond Insurer, if applicable, if it is not in 
default in its payment obligation under the related bond insurance policy; and 
(d) by action or suit in equity enjoin any acts or things which may be unlawful or in 
violation of the rights of the Bondholders and the Bond Insurer , if it is not in default in its payment 
obligation under the related bond insurance policy; and 
(e) exercise all or all other rights and remedies provided for by t he Act or by any 
other law or in equity; and 
(f) exercise all rights and remedies granted under the Lease. 
Discontinuance of Proceedings by Trustee. If any proceeding tak en by the 2018B Bonds Trustee 
on account of any default is discontinued or is determined adversely to the 2018B Bonds Trustee, the 
Authority, the 2018B Bonds Trustee, the Bond Insurer, if applicable, and the Bondholders shall be 
restored to their former positions and rights under the 2018B B onds Indenture as though no such 
proceeding had been taken. 
The Holders of a majority in aggregate principal amount of the Bonds then Outstanding (with the 
consent of the Bond Insurer, if applicable, if it is not in def ault in its payment obligation under the related 
bond insurance policy), have the right to direct the method and  place of conducting all remedial 
proceedings by the 2018B Bonds T rustee, provided that the 2018B  Bonds Trustee is given indemnity 
satisfactory to it, that such direction will not be in conflict  with any rule of law or with the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture, and that the 2018B Bonds Trustee shall have the righ t to decline to follow any such direction 
which in the opinion of the 2018B Bonds Trustee would be unjustly prejudicial to Bondholders not 
parties to such direction and will not unduly prejudice the rights of Bondholders. 
No Bondholder will have the right to pursue any remedy under th e 2018B Bonds Indenture, 
unless (i) an Event of Default has occurred of which the 2018B Bonds Trustee has been given written 
notice as provided in the 2018B Bonds Indenture; (ii) the Holders of at least 25% in aggregate principal 
amount of the Bonds then Outstandi ng have made a written reques t to the 2018B Bonds Trustee to 
exercise the powers granted in the 2018B Bonds Indenture or to pursue such remedy in its or their name 
or names; (iii) the 2018B Bonds Trustee has; been offered indem nity satisfactory to it against costs, 
expenses and liabilities, as provided in the 2018B Bonds Indenture; (iv) the 2018B Bonds Trustee 
thereafter fails to comply with such request within a reasonabl e time; and (v) the Bond Insurer (if  E-14 
  
applicable) has given its written consent if it is not in defau lt in its payment obligations under related 
bond insurance policy. 
Any moneys received by the 2018B Bonds Trustee in connection wi th an Event of Default under 
the 2018B Bonds Indenture are applied in the following order: 
Before any amounts are paid pursuant to the provisions describe d in the following subparagraphs, 
such moneys shall first be applied to the payment of the costs and expenses of the 2018B Bonds Trustee, 
including reasonable fees and expenses of counsel and all costs and disbursements allowed by a court if 
there be any court action. 
Unless the principal of all the Bonds shall have become or shal l have been declared due and 
payable, all such moneys shall be applied: 
First - To the payment to the persons entitled thereto of all i nstallments 
of interest then due on the Bonds, in order of the maturity of the installments of 
such interest and, if the amount available shall not be suffici ent to pay in full any 
particular installment, then to the payment ratably, according to the amounts due 
on such installment to the persons entitled thereto, without an y discrimination or 
privilege; and  
Second - To the payment to the persons entitled thereto of the unpaid 
principal of any of the Bonds which shall have become due (othe r than Bonds 
called for redemption for the payment of which moneys are held pursuant to the 
provisions of the 2018B Bonds Indenture), in the order of their due dates, with 
interest on such Bonds from the respective dates on which they become due and, 
if the amount available shall not be sufficient to pay in full the Bonds due on any 
particular date, together with such interest, then to the payment ratably, according 
to the amount of principal due on such date, to the persons ent itled thereto 
without any discrimination or privilege. 
If the principal of all the Bonds shall have become due or shal l have been declared due and 
payable, all such moneys shall be applied to the payment of the principal and interest then due and unpaid 
on the Bonds, without preference or priority of principal over interest or of interest over principal, or of 
any installment of interest over any other installment of inter est, or of any Bond over any other Bond, and 
interest to the persons entitled thereto without any discrimination or privilege. 
If the principal of all the Bonds shall have been declared due and payable, and if such declaration 
shall thereafter have been rescinded and annulled under the pro visions of the 2018B Bonds Indenture 
then, subject to the provisions described in the immediately pr eceding subparagraph above (in the event 
that the principal of all the Bonds shall later become due or be declared due and payable), the money shall 
be applied in accordance with the provisions described above (i n the event that the principal of all the 
Bonds has not become due or declared due and payable). 
Trustee May Enforce Rights Without Possession of Bonds. All rig hts under the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture and the Bonds may be enforced by the 2018B Bonds Trus tee without the possession of any 
Bonds or the production thereof at the trial or other proceedings relative thereto, and any proceeding 
instituted by the 2018B Bonds Trustee shall be brought in its name for the equal and ratable benefit of the 
holders of the Bonds.  E-15 
  
Remedies Not Exclusive. No remedy conferred within the 2018B Bonds Indenture is intended to 
be exclusive of any other remedy or remedies, and each remedy is in addition to every other remedy given 
under the 2018B Bonds Indenture as of delivery thereof or there after existing at law or in equity or by 
statute. 
Delays and Omissions Not to Impair Rights. No delay or omission  in respect of exercising any 
right or power accruing upon any Event of Default shall impair such right or power or be a waiver of such 
Event of Default, and every remedy given by the 2018B Bonds Ind enture may be exercised from time to 
time and as often as may be deemed expedient. 
The 2018B Bonds Trustee 
Notice of Default: Right to Investigate. The 2018B Bonds Truste e shall, within thirty (30) days 
after the occurrence of an Event of Default described under cla uses (a), (b) or (c) under the subcaption 
“Events of Default Defined” above, and in the case of any other  Event of Default within sixty (60) days 
after it has knowledge of such other Event of Default hereunder, give written notice by first class mail to 
the Registered Owners of the Bonds of all defaults, unless such  defaults have been remedied, not 
including any notice or periods of grace provided for therein; provided that, except in the case of a default 
in payment of principal or sinking fund payments on the Bonds o r interest on the Bonds, the 2018B 
Bonds Trustee may withhold such notice as long as it in good fa ith determines that such withholding is in 
the interest of the Bondholders. The 2018B Bonds Trustee shall not be deemed to have notice of any 
Event of Default described under clauses (d) through (f) under the subcaption “Events of Default 
Defined” above unless the 2018B Bonds Trustee shall have  actual knowledge therefor or unless notified 
in writing of such Event of Default by the holders of at least 25% in aggregate principal amount of the 
Bonds then Outstanding. The 2018B  Bonds Trustee may, however, a t any time require of the Authority 
full information as to the performance of any covenant under th e 2018B Bonds Indenture; and if 
information satisfactory to it is not forthcoming, the 2018B Bo nds Trustee may make or cause to be 
made, at the expense of the Authority, an investigation into th e affairs of the Authority related to the 
2018B Bonds Indenture and the properties covered thereby. 
Obligation to Act on Defaults. If any Event of Default shall ha ve occurred and be continuing, the 
2018B Bonds Trustee shall exercise such of the rights and remed ies vested in it by the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture and shall use the same degree of care in their exerci se as a prudent man would exercise or use 
in the circumstances in the conduct of his own affairs; provided, that if in the opinion of the 2018B Bonds 
Trustee such action may tend to involve expense or liability, i t shall not be obligated to take such action 
unless it is furnished with indemnity satisfactory to it. Nothi ng described under this subcaption 
“Obligation to Act on Defaults” shall be construed to require t he 2018B Bonds Trustee to exercise any 
such rights and remedies where to do so would cause the 2018B B onds Trustee to violate any law or any 
order of a court of competent jurisdiction. 
Trustee May Deal in and Own Bonds. The 2018B Bonds Trustee may in good faith buy, sell, 
own, hold, and deal in any of the Bonds and may join in any act ion which any Bondholders may be 
entitled to take with like effect as if the 2018B Bonds Trustee were not a party to the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture. The 2018B Bonds Trustee may also engage in or be interested in any financial or other 
transaction with the Authority, the City (and the Bond Insurer (if applicable) provided that if the 2018B 
Bonds Trustee determines that any such relation is in conflict with its duties under the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture, it shall eliminate the conflict or resign as Trustee. 
Resignation of Trustee. The 2018B Bonds Trustee may resign and be discharged of the trusts 
created by the 2018B Bonds Indenture by written resignation fil ed with the Secretary of the Authority not 
less than sixty (60) days before the date when such resignation  is to take effect; provided notice of such  E-16 
  
resignation is mailed by first c lass mail, postage prepaid, to Bondholders not less than three weeks prior 
to the date when the resignation is to take effect. Such resign ation shall take effect on the day specified 
therein unless a successor trustee is previously appointed, in which event the resignation shall take effect 
immediately on the appointment of such successor. 
If no appointment of a successor shall be made within sixty (60 ) days after notice is given by the 
2018B Bonds Trustee in accordance with the 2018B Bonds Indenture, the 2018B Bonds Trustee or any 
Bondholder may apply to any court of competent jurisdiction for  the appointment of a successor. 
Thereupon, such court, after such notice as it may deem proper and may prescribe, may appoint a 
successor Trustee. 
Removal of Trustee. Any Trustee under the 2018B Bonds Indenture  may be removed, upon sixty 
(60) days’ notice, at any time by an instrument appointing a su ccessor to the 2018B Bonds Trustee so 
removed, executed by the holders of a majority in aggregate pri ncipal amount of the Bonds then 
Outstanding and filed with the 2018B Bonds Trustee and the Authority. 
Appointment of Successor Trustee. If the 2018B Bonds Trustee or  any successor trustee resigns 
or is removed or dissolved, or if its property or business is t aken under the control of any state or federal 
court or administrative body, a vacancy shall forthwith exist in the office of the 2018B Bonds Trustee, 
and the Authority shall appoint a successor and shall mail prom ptly notice of such appointment to the 
Registered Owners of the Bonds. If the Authority fails to make such appointment within thirty (30) days, 
the holders of a majority in principal amount of the Bonds then Outstanding may do so. 
Qualification of Successor. A successor trustee shall be a nati onal bank with trust powers or a 
bank and trust company or a trust company having capital and surplus of at least $50,000,000. 
Amendments and Supplements  
The Authority and the 2018B Bonds Trustee may, without the cons ent of any of the Holders of 
the Bonds, enter into a Supplemental Indenture for one or more of the following purposes: 
(a) to set forth any or all of the matters required in connection w ith the issuance of 
Additional Bonds under the 2018B Bonds Indenture; 
(b) to add additional covenants of the Authority or to surrender an y right or power 
conferred upon the Authority under the 2018B Bonds Indenture; 
(c) to cure any ambiguity, inconsiste ncy, or omission or to cure, c orrect or 
supplement any provision of the 2018B Bonds Indenture in such m anner as will not be inconsistent with 
the 2018B Bonds Indenture and shall not impair the security the reof or adversely affect the Bondholders; 
or 
(d) to preserve the exemption from federal income tax of interest on the Bonds.  
Exclusive of supplemental indentures for the purposes set forth  in the preceding paragraphs, the 
2018B Bonds Indenture may be amended from time to time by a Sup plemental Indenture approved by the 
Bond Insurer (if applicable) and the Holders of at least a majo rity in aggregate principal amount of the 
Bonds then Outstanding; provided that no amendment shall be mad e which adversely affects one or more 
but less than all series of Bonds without the consent of the Ho lders of at least a majority in aggregate 
principal amount of the then outstanding Bonds of each series s o affected, and no amendment shall be 
made which affects the rights of some but less than all the out standing Bonds of any series without the  E-17 
  
consent of the Bond Insurer and the Holders of a majority in ag gregate principal amount of the Bonds so 
affected; and except with respect to: 
(a) the interest on and principal payable on any Bonds; 
(b) the dates of maturity or the redemption provisions of any Bonds; and 
(c) Article XIII of the 2018B Bonds Indenture (regarding amendments  a n d  
supplements thereto). 
Amendments with respect to items (a), (b) and (c) in the preced ing paragraph shall be effective 
only with the consent of the Bond Insurer (if applicable) and t he Holders of all Bonds Outstanding of the 
series which would be affected by such amendments. 
Amendment of Lease 
 If the Authority and the City p ropose to amend the Lease in su ch manner as would adversely 
affect the interests of Bondholders and, therefore, would requi re the consent of the 2018B Bonds Trustee 
as provided in the 2018B Bonds Indenture, the 2018B Bonds Trustee shall notify the Bond Insurer and 
the Bondholders of the proposed amendment and may consent there to with the consent of the Bond 
Insurer and the holders of at least a majority in aggregate pri ncipal amount of the Bonds Outstanding 
which would be affected by the action proposed to be taken; provided, that the 2018B Bonds Trustee shall 
not, without the unanimous consent of the holders of all Bonds then Outstanding, consent to any 
amendment which would (1) decrease the amounts payable under the Lease, (2) change the dates of 
payment or change any of the prepayment provisions of the Lease , or (3) change the sections of the Lease 
captioned “Rental Payments,” No Abatement or Setoff,” “Rentals to be Net,” “Termination,” “Event of 
Default” and “No Personal Recourses; Indemnification of Authority.” 
Discharge of Lien of Indenture 
If the Authority deposits with the 2018B Bonds Trustee funds su fficient to pay the principal of or 
redemption price of any Bonds becoming due at maturity, by call for redemption, or otherwise, together 
with interest accrued to the due date, interest on such bonds w ill cease to accrue on the due date, and 
thereafter the Holders of the Bonds will be restricted to the funds so deposited as provided in the 2018B 
Bonds Indenture. 
When interest on, and principal or redemption price of. all Bon ds issued under the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture have been paid, or there shall have been deposited wi th the 2018B Bonds Trustee an amount of 
cash and/or non-callable Government Obligations; the principal of and interest on which, when due, will 
provide sufficient moneys without reinvestment to pay in full t he principal of and interest on the Bonds, 
then the right, title and interest of the 2018B Bonds Trustee i n the Pledged Revenues and in the Lease 
shall cease and terminate. 
Miscellaneous Provisions 
No Personal Recourse. No personal recourse shall be had for any  claim based on the 2018B 
Bonds Indenture or the Bonds against any member, officer, emplo yee or agent, past, present or future, of 
the Authority or of any successor body as such, either directly  or through the Authority or any such 
successor body, under any constitutional provision, statute or rule of law or by the enforcement of any 
assessment or penalty or otherwise, alleging personal liability  on the part of such person unless such 
claims are based upon the gross negligence or willful misconduct of such person.  E-18 
  
The Bonds are payable solely from the Pledged Revenues, and any  o t h e r  m o n e y s  h e l d  b y  t h e  
2018B Bonds Trustee under the 2018B Bonds Indenture for such pu rpose. There shall be no other 
recourse under the Bonds, the 2018B Bonds Indenture (except for the compensation and indemnity 
provisions for the 2018B Bonds Trustee), the Lease or otherwise  against the Authority or any other 
property owned by it at the time of the delivery of the 2018B Bonds Indenture or thereafter. The 
Authority shall be conclusively deemed to have complied with all of its covenants and other obligations 
under the 2018B Bonds Indenture, upon requiring the City in the Lease to agree to perform such 
Authority covenants and other obligations (excepting only any a pprovals or consents permitted or 
required to be given by the Authority under the 2018B Bonds Indenture, and any exceptions to the 
performance by the City of the A uthority’s covenants and other obligations under the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture, as may be contained in the Lease or such covenants which can be performed only by the 
Authority). However, nothing c ontained in the Lease shall preve nt the Authority from time to time, in its 
discretion, from performing any such covenants or other obligat ions. The Authority shall have no liability 
for any failure to fulfill, or breach by the City of, the City’s obligations under the Bonds, the 2018B 
Bonds Indenture, the Lease, or otherwise, including without lim itation the City’s obligation to fulfill the 
Authority’s covenants and other obligations under the 2018B Bonds Indenture. 
The Bonds issued under the 2018B Bonds Indenture shall be limited obligations of the Authority 
payable solely from the rentals due under the Lease and as othe rwise provided under the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture. The liability of the Authority under the Lease shall  be enforceable solely from the Leased 
Premises and there shall be no other recourse against any other assets of the Authority. 
Deposit of Funds for Payment of Bonds. If the Authority deposit s with the 2018B Bonds Trustee 
funds sufficient to pay the principal or Redemption Price of any Bonds becoming due, either at maturity 
or by call for redemption or otherwise, together with all inter est accruing thereon to the due date, interest 
on the Bonds shall cease to accrue on the due date and all liab ility of the Authority with respect to such 
Bonds, except as hereinafter described. Thereafter the holders of such Bonds shall be restricted 
exclusively to the funds so deposited for any claim of whatsoev er nature with respect to such Bonds, and 
the 2018B Bonds Trustee shall hold such funds in trust for such  holders. Moneys so deposited with the 
2018B Bonds Trustee which remain unclaimed five years after the  date payment thereof becomes due 
shall, upon request of the Authority, if the Authority is not at the time, to the knowledge of the 2018B 
Bonds Trustee, in default with respect to any covenant in the 2 018B Bonds Indenture or the Bonds, be 
paid to the Authority for and on account of the City; and the h olders of the Bonds for which the deposit 
was made shall thereafter be limited to a claim against the Authority. 
 
   E-19 
  
THE LEASE 
Term; Rentals 
The Lease, dated as of November 1, 1996, has been entered into between the Authority, as 
Lessor, and the City, as Lessee. The Lease provides that the Au thority shall lease to the City the Leased 
Premises for a term beginning as of November 1, 1996 and ending  thirty (30) years from the execution 
date, subject to extension or termination as set forth therein. 
If the Bonds are outstanding on the date scheduled for expirati on of the Lease, the term of the 
Lease shall be automatically extended for such time as may be necessary, plus thirty (30) days, to enable 
the Authority to comply with any covenant, condition, term or provision of the 2018B Bonds Indenture, 
or to exercise any of the remedies provided to the Authority therein. 
The City shall pay as rentals to the Authority or its assigns t he following sums at the following 
times: 
(a) for deposit in the Bond Fund on o r before 10:00a.m. Philadelphi a time, on the 
date five (5) Business Days prior to each Interest Payment Date  or Sinking Fund Installment Date (as 
defined in the 2018B Bonds Indent ure) or on any other date that  principal of, and premium, if any, and 
interest on the Bonds, or any portion thereof, shall be due and  payable, whether by maturity, redemption 
(other than by acceleration under the 2018B Bonds Indenture) or  otherwise, an amount which, together 
with other moneys in the Bond Fund available therefor, is suffi cient to make the Authority’s required 
payments of principal of., premium, if any, and interest on the  Bonds then becoming due, whether by 
maturity, redemption (other than by acceleration under the 2018 B Bonds Indenture) or otherwise until the 
principal of, or redemption premium, if any, and interest on th e Bonds shall have been paid in full or 
provision for the payment there of shall have been made in accor dance with the provisions of the 2018B 
Bonds Indenture; and 
(b) additionally, from time to time,  the City shall be required to make payments 
necessary to make up any deficiency in the Funds established un der the 2018B Bonds Indenture, 
including an amount which is sufficient to make any payment required to be made by the Authority to the 
United States Treasury as provided in the 2018B Bonds Indenture, as determined and specified in writing 
by the City, other than as stated above, and to make payments to reimburse the Authority for any 
premiums paid for insurance policies purchased pursuant to the Lease to the extent that the City has funds 
appropriated for such purposes in such Fiscal Year. The Authori ty shall advise the 2018B Bonds Trustee 
of the amount of such rental paym ents (other than deficiencies in any Funds established under the 2018B 
Bonds Indenture) in sufficient time for the 2018B Bonds Trustee to give such notice to the City by 
January 1 of each calendar year. 
All rental payments described under subparagraph (a) above shall be paid in immediately 
available funds and held, invested, disbursed and applied as provided in the 2018B Bonds Indenture. 
In the Lease, the Authority directs the rent (except such amoun ts as shall provide for certain 
indemnification which shall be paid directly to the Authority) to be paid directly to the 2018B Bonds 
Trustee. 
If the moneys available to the Authority to meet its requiremen ts under the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture or under the Bonds are insufficient to pay the Authority’s obligations thereunder, then the City, 
upon demand, shall pay to the Authority or its assigns such add itional sum or sums, in each Fiscal Year, 
as shall be required for such purposes, subject to the provisions of the following paragraph.  E-20 
  
The rentals shall be payable only out of the current revenues o f the City, or funds otherwise 
available for payment of the rentals, and the City agrees to pr ovide for the payment of rentals and include 
the same in its annual Operating Budget for each Fiscal Year. I f the current revenues arc insufficient to 
pay the total rentals in any Fiscal Year as the same become due and payable, the City covenants to include 
amounts not so paid in its Operating Budget for the ensuing Fis cal Year and to produce sufficient current 
revenues to pay in each ensuing Fiscal Year such balance due in  addition to the amount of rentals due for 
such ensuing Fiscal Year. The failure of the City to make any p ayment required by the rental payment 
provisions of the Lease for a period of five (5) Business Days after the date specified for payment shall 
constitute a default under the Lease. An event of default under the Lea se does not cause an acceleration 
of the payments under the Lease. 
The City covenants to make appropriations in each of its Fiscal  Years in such amounts as shall be 
required in order to make all rental payments when due and paya ble under the Lease in each of the City’s 
Fiscal Years. 
General 
The City shall pay rentals and additional sums required under t he Lease without suspension or 
abatement of any nature, notwithstanding that all or any part o f the Leased Premises shall have been 
wholly or partially destroyed, damaged or injured and shall not  have been repaired, replaced or rebuilt So 
long as any of the Bonds remain outstanding or sufficient money  for the payment of the Bonds in full, 
including principal, interest or Redemption Price, if any. shal l not be held by the 2018B Bonds Trustee in 
trust therefor, the obligation of the City to pay rentals shall  be an absolute, unconditional, general 
obligation of the City and shall not be suspended, abated, reduced, abrogated, waived, diminished or 
otherwise modified in any manner or to any extent whatsoever. 
All risk of loss, damage, theft or destruction of the Leased Pr emises shall be borne by the City. 
The City shall pay all applicable license fees, assessments, le vies, charges, utility service charges and 
taxes with respect to the Leased Premises and agrees to assume liability for and indemnify, protect, save 
and keep the Authority harmless from and against any and all li abilities or claims imposed on, asserted 
against or incurred by the Authority in any way relating to or arising out of the Lease or the use of the 
Leased Premises, unless the losses, damages or liabilities arise from bad faith, fraud, deceit, negligence or 
willful misconduct of a member, officer or employee of the Authority. 
Additional Covenants of the City 
In addition, the City covenants in the Lease to properly maintain the Leased Premises. 
Further, the City makes certain covenants with respect to maint aining the federal tax-exempt 
status of the Bonds, including, without limitation: 
(1) that it will neither make nor instruct the 2018B Bonds Trustee to make 
any investment or other use of the proceeds of the Bonds which would cause the Bonds to be “arbitrage 
bonds” as that term is defined in Section 148(a) of the Code, and all applicable regulations promulgated 
with respect thereto and that it will comply with the requireme nts of the Code throughout the term of the 
Bonds so that the interest on the Bonds shall be excluded from gross income of the holders thereof for 
federal income tax purposes; and 
(2) that it will not use or permit the use of the Leased Premises i n such a 
manner as would result, or allow the use of the Leased Premises  by any entity whose use thereof would  E-21 
  
result, in the loss of the exclusion of interest on the Bonds from gross income of the holders thereof for 
federal income tax purposes. 
Destruction, Damage, and Eminent Domain 
If the Leased Premises shall be wholly or partially destroyed o r damaged by fire or other casualty 
covered by insurance or shall be wholly or partially condemned, taken or injured by any person, including 
any person possessing the right to exercise the power of or a p ower in the nature of eminent domain or 
transferred to such a person by way of a conveyance in lieu of the exercise of such a power by such a 
person, the Authority and the C ity covenant that they will take  all actions and will do all things which 
may be necessary to enable recovery to be made upon such polici es of insurance or self-insurance, if 
applicable, or on account of such taking, condemnation, conveya nce, damage or injury in order that 
moneys due on account of losses suffered may be collected and p aid to the 2018B Bonds Trustee.  The 
2018B Bonds Trustee is authorized, in its own name, as trustee of an express trust, to demand, collect, 
sue, settle claims, receipt and release moneys which may be due  and payable under such policies of 
insurance or on account of such condemnations, damage or injury. 
Any appraisement or adjustment of loss or damage and any settlement or payment therefor, which 
may be agreed upon by the Authority, the City and the appropria te insurer, if any, or condemnor or 
person, shall be evidenced to the 2018B Bonds Trustee by the ce rtificate and approvals set forth in the 
2018B Bonds Indenture.  The 2018B Bonds Trustee may rely conclusively upon such certificates. 
Notice of Property Loss 
Immediately after occurrence of loss or damage in excess of $75 0,000 covered by insurance, or 
after notice of condemnation has been received, or the occurren ce of injury or damage, the City shall 
notify in writing the Authority, the Consulting Engineer and th e 2018B Bonds Trustee thereof and said 
Consulting Engineer promptly shall determine and advise the Authority, the 2018B Bonds Trustee and the 
City, in writing, whether it is practicable and desirable to re pair, reconstruct or replace such damaged or 
destroyed or condemned property.  If the Consulting Engineer sh all advise in the affirmative and the City 
and the Authority agree thereto in writing, the Authority or th e City, as appropriate, shall proceed 
forthwith with repair, reconstruction or replacement, to the extent of insurance proceeds or sums received 
as condemnation or other damages  collected with respect to such loss or damage, in the manner provided 
in the Lease and in the 2018B Bonds Indenture. The City shall pay from its own funds for the cost of such 
repair, reconstruction or replacement to the extent insurance proceeds or other sums received with respect 
to such loss or damage, together with any sums advanced for the  Acquisition Fund pursuant to the 2018B 
Bonds Indenture, shall be insufficient. 
Disposition of Insurance Proceeds – Property Damage 
If moneys collected under policies of insurance or on account of condemnation, damage or injury, 
with respect to any one loss shall equal or exceed $750,000 and  the Consulting Engineer shall have 
determined that repair, reconstruction or replacement is practicable and desirable, and if the Authority and 
the City shall request, the Authority agrees that such moneys s hall be paid by the 2018B Bonds Trustee 
for costs of such repair, reconstruction or replacement as provided in the 2018B Bonds Indenture from the 
Acquisition Fund.  Moneys in excess of the amount needed for co sts of such repair, reconstruction or 
replacement shall be deposited in the Bond Fund. 
If moneys collected under the policies of insurance or on account of condemnation, damage or 
injury with respect to any one loss shall be less than $750,000  and the Consulting Engineer shall have 
determined that repair, reconstruction or replacement is practicable and desirable, and if the Authority and  E-22 
  
the City shall request, the Authority agrees that the 2018B Bon ds Trustee shall pay such moneys to the 
City to be used by it for the purpose of paying costs of such repair, reconstruction or replacement and 
neither requisition nor certificates shall be required. 
If any insurance or condemnation proceeds are not applied as de scribed above within 12 months 
after receipt by the 2018B Bonds Trustee, or if the Authority f ails to obtain, or cause to be obtained the 
required certificate within three months after occurrence of th e condemnation, injury or damage to the 
Leased Premises, or if the certificate states that the repair, reconstruction or replacement is not practicable 
or desirable, the insurance proceeds or condemnation, awards sh all be deposited in the Bond Fund for 
application to the extraordinary redemption of the Bonds in acc ordance with the provisions of the 2018B 
Bonds Indenture and the Bonds. 
Events of Default; Termination of the Lease 
If the City shall fail to make any rental payment required by the Lease or by the 2018B Bonds 
Indenture when the same shall become due and payable and if suc h failure continues for five (5) Business 
Days thereafter, or if the City shall fail to perform any of it s other covenants, agreements or obligations 
contained in the Lease and such failure continues for thirty (3 0) days (or such longer period as may be 
permitted by the Lease) after the Authority or the 2018B Bonds Trustee gives the City written notice 
thereof, or the City shall file or has filed against it a petit ion of bankruptcy or for arrangement or for 
reorganization pursuant to the Federal Bankruptcy Act or other similar laws. Federal or state, or if, by the 
decree of a court of competent jurisdiction, the City is adjudi cated bankrupt or declared insolvent, or 
makes an assignment for the bene fit of creditors, or admits in writing its inability to pay its debts 
generally when or as they become due. or consents to the appoin tment of a trustee, receiver or liquidation 
of all or any part of its assets or revenues: provided that, if  any such proceeding is commenced by a 
person other than the City, the re shall be no Event of Default if such proceedings are dismissed within 
sixty (60) days of the filing of the initial pleadings therein,  then the same shall constitute an “Event of 
Default” under the terms of the L ease. Thereafter, while such E vent of Default is continuing, the 
Authority may, with the prior written consent of the Bond Insur er (if applicable), terminate the Lease and 
take such other acts or steps as may be available under the Lease or in law, equity or otherwise. 
Termination of the Lease by the Authority shall not relieve the  City of its obligations under the 
Lease. 
Amendment of the Lease 
Subject to the provision that the parties shall not adversely a ffect the rights or the security of the 
Bondholders or of the Bond Insurer (if applicable), the parties  to the Lease may amend the Lease for only 
the following purposes: 
(a) to cure any ambiguity, defect or omission therein or in any amendment thereto or 
to supplement any provision thereof; or 
(b) to reflect a change in applicable law, which shall not impair t he security of the 
Lease or adversely affect the rights of the Bondholders or the Bond Insurer (if applicable); or 
(c) to provide for additional rent as provided in the Lease in connection with the 
issuance of Additional Bonds; or 
(d) to add to the covenants and agreements of the City therein cont ained, or to 
surrender any right or power therein reserved to or conferred u pon the City which shall not impair the  E-23 
  
security thereof or adversely affect the rights of the Bondhold ers or of the Bond Insurer (if applicable) 
under the 2018B Bonds Indenture. 
All other amendments must be a pproved by the 2018B Bonds Truste e and the Bond Insurer (if 
applicable) and. if the 2018B Bonds Indenture must be amended w ith the Bondholders’ consent, the 
Bondholders in the same manner and to the same extent as set forth in the 2018B Bonds Indenture.
